text.title: Continuous Delivery (Index) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the continuous-delivery methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: If any CD prerequisite (CI, tests, IaC, flags) is missing, load cd-basics first; do not jump to cd-pipelines.
rule.rationale: Concrete YAML without prerequisites is broken on day 1.

rule.statement: If all CD prerequisites are satisfied, load cd-pipelines for concrete YAML + strategy.
rule.rationale: Mature teams need the YAML, not the principles lecture.

rule.statement: Use this methodology only when the next step is unclear; otherwise load the child directly.
rule.rationale: Routing adds latency; skip it when the choice is known.

rule.statement: If deploy frequency < weekly, start with cd-basics. If ≥ daily or pending pipeline edit, load cd-pipelines.
rule.rationale: DORA frequency band correlates with which child carries more value.

rule.statement: Output is a decision record naming the next methodology to load + rationale.
rule.rationale: Visible decision record prevents accidental re-routing.

rule.statement: Skip this router if the next child is already known, or CD is not in scope, or workload is batch.
